title: Software Update - DFU - Headset at low battery - Software update tab in device settings
scenario: DFU is blocked with pop-up when headset battery is below 5%, becomes available when plugged into charger, and is re-blocked if unplugged during update
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and connect to the Poly Bluetooth headset.
3. Ensure the headset battery level is below 5% and NOT connected to a charger.
4. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update (or DFU section).
5. Attempt to initiate a firmware update.
7. Connect the headset to a charger while battery remains below 5%.
8. Attempt to initiate a firmware update again.
10. While the update is in progress (after step 8), unplug the headset from the charger while battery is still below 5%.
11. Observe the app response during the update process.
expected: 1. When headset battery is below 5% and not charging, the app should display a pop-up: "Update currently not available, please try back later" and block the firmware update. 2. When the headset is plugged into a charger (battery still below 5%), the firmware update should become available and proceed normally. 3. If the headset is unplugged during the update while battery is still below 5%, the update should be blocked again with the same pop-up message.
